# UC-17.1.67 · Cisco AI Endpoint Behavioural (AEB) Profile-Drift and Behaviour-Anomaly Detection

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*ISE notices if a device suddenly starts acting unusually — like a printer that suddenly tries to talk to email servers. We surface these unusual changes for the security team to investigate.*

---

## Description

Detects endpoint behaviour-pattern anomalies (traffic class drift, time-of-use anomaly, peer-set change) reported by Cisco AI Endpoint Behavioural analytics.

## Value

Behaviour anomalies are hard to detect manually. AEB models identify when an endpoint suddenly starts behaving differently — often the earliest sign of compromise. Surfacing these in Splunk feeds the SOC's risk model.

## Implementation

Schedule every 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Simulate behaviour change on an endpoint (e.g. abrupt traffic-pattern shift). AEB emits 80201. UC-17.1.67 fires.

### Negative scenario

Steady behaviour. No fires.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (80201, 80202)
| stats count by endpoint_mac, anomaly_type
| where count > 0
```

## Visualization

Top-N anomaly types; per-endpoint anomaly history.

## Known False Positives

- Holiday / off-hours pattern shifts.
- New software/job role changing endpoint behaviour.

## References

- [Cisco AI Endpoint Analytics — Behavioural Insights](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/products/cloud-systems-management/dna-spaces/ai-endpoint-analytics.html)
